rationale: The citing paper’s core mechanism is listening-versus-reading effects on selective attention, arousal, and valence-weighted recall in news interpretation, whereas the cited paper examines acoustic pitch effects on perceived product size in advertising. This suggests only a broad auditory-perception overlap, and the provided match evidence is just a DOI reference entry with no in-text citation context. Given the sparse linkage evidence, it is not safe to drop but also not clearly important enough to keep outright.
